fields.summary: Adding support for moving apps between queues in Capacity Scheduler
YARN Admission Control/Planner: enhancing the resource allocation model with time.

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: As discussed with [~leftnoteasy] and [~jianhe], we are breaking up YARN-1707 to smaller patches for manageability. This JIRA will address adding support for moving apps between queues in Capacity Scheduler.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Capacity Scheduler changes for moving apps between queues

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body:
I am attaching a patch that has the following updates:
   
   * Add moveApplication() and moveAllApps() to ResourceSchedulerAPI.
   * Implementation of above APIs for Capacity Scheduler
   * Update RMAppImpl state machine to handle Move app event correctly. This is required especially if we use move programmatically as is done by the reservation system as part of YARN-1051. Essentially we need to handle Move event when app is in NEW_SAVING state and add it ignore list for other missing states like FINISHED, KILLED, FAILED etc that are tracked by scheduler
   * Test cases for testing moveApplication() and moveAllApps()

Let me know your thoughts.

This message is automatically generated.
[~subru] have you looked at YARN-2248? It also allows you to move apps between queues in CapacityScheduler.

Thanks [~vvasudev] for pointing out the parallel work and [~leftnoteasy] for your feedback.

I agree we should merge both & have a [proposal | https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-2248?focusedCommentId=14088137] based on your review.

About your comments on the patch I uploaded:
  * Thanks for clarifying on not requiring to check capacity before move.
  * I will look at the implementing state store in move transition.
  * Will look at the test failures & fix them, my bad.

I am uploading an updated patch that merges all the test cases that [~keyki] wrote as part of YARN-2248. Thanks for the test cases especially the queue metrics one makes this a more robust patch.

[~leftnoteasy]
All the test cases run for me. I looked at the report and they all fail with a single exception: "java.net.UnknownHostException: asf901.ygridcore.net: asf901.ygridcore.net" which is unrelated to this patch. 
Only TestClientRMTokens fails for me but with "can't access Kerberos realm" which I assume is due to incomplete local kerb setup/configuration and I do not see that issue in Jenkins.

Implementing state store for move is being tracked separately by YARN-1558. I'll be glad to help with that post YARN-1051.

Hi [~subru],
It's good to have more test from YARN-2248, now I think it covers most cases,
Several comments about tests:
1) testMoveAppForMoveToQueueCannotRunApp:
I think the name may not be precise enough. Actually, it means moving an app from a small queue (cannot allocate more resource) to a larger queue. I suggest you can change the name
And the comment is incorrect:
{code}
        +    // task_0_0 task_1_0 allocated, used=4G 
        +    nodeUpdate(nm_0); 
{code}
The used should = 2G here.

2) testMoveAllApps:
bq. +    Thread.sleep(100);
I think we don't need sleep here, moveApplication is a synchronized call.

Thanks,
Wangda

Thanks [~vvasudev] for resolving the host issue. The only test case that failed - TestAMRestart passes consistently for me.

Thanks for your feedback [~leftnoteasy]. I am uploading a new patch that addresses all your comments. 

Additionally based on our offline discussion and comments in YARN-807, I have added pending apps also in CapacityScheduler#getAppsInQueue() and refactored moveAllApps into AbstractYarnScheduler. I ran all the relevant test cases and things look good.

This message is automatically generated.

[~subru],
I've ran the previous failed test locally, it passed. And as same as the latest Jenkins result. 
I think LGTM, +1.
[~jianhe], would you like to take a look at this?

Thanks,
Wangda
Noticed that SchedulerApplication#setQueue should be updated as well when move happens. (can you add test for this too)

[~jianhe], thanks for your detailed comments. I am attaching a patch that addresses it.

Summary of changes:
   * getCheckLeafQueue renamed to getAndCheckLeafQueue
   * Add test case to move app to a sibling queue within the same parent
   * Removed unnecessary unreserve as we need to only the metrics which is already being done in SchedulerApplicationAttempt as you right pointed out.
   * Removed unwanted synchronized block
   * Updated SchedulerApplication#setQueue (nice catch). Added checks in test case. Also updated AppSchedulingInfo as it also maintains the queue name.

patch looks good overall,  just one thing that we can move "metrics.submitApp(userName);" to addApplication() so that we can avoid changing the method signature to include the isMove flag.
Good suggestion [~jianhe]. Uploading an updated pitch that has the fix.
looks good to me , resubmitting the same patch to kick jenkins
Will rename and resubmit patch in the hope that will simulate Jenkins.

Hi [~subru],
It's good to have more test from YARN-2248, now I think it covers most cases,
Several comments about tests:
1) testMoveAppForMoveToQueueCannotRunApp:
I think the name may not be precise enough. Actually, it means moving an app from a small queue (cannot allocate more resource) to a larger queue. I suggest you can change the name
And the comment is incorrect:
{code}
        +    // task_0_0 task_1_0 allocated, used=4G 
        +    nodeUpdate(nm_0); 
{code}
The used should = 2G here.

2) testMoveAllApps:
bq. +    Thread.sleep(100);
I think we don't need sleep here, moveApplication is a synchronized call.

Thanks,
Wangda

Thanks [~vvasudev] for resolving the host issue. The only test case that failed - TestAMRestart passes consistently for me.

Thanks for your feedback [~leftnoteasy]. I am uploading a new patch that addresses all your comments. 

Additionally based on our offline discussion and comments in YARN-807, I have added pending apps also in CapacityScheduler#getAppsInQueue() and refactored moveAllApps into AbstractYarnScheduler. I ran all the relevant test cases and things look good.

[~jianhe], thanks for your detailed comments. I am attaching a patch that addresses it.

Summary of changes:
   * getCheckLeafQueue renamed to getAndCheckLeafQueue
   * Add test case to move app to a sibling queue within the same parent
   * Removed unnecessary unreserve as we need to only the metrics which is already being done in SchedulerApplicationAttempt as you right pointed out.
   * Removed unwanted synchronized block
   * Updated SchedulerApplication#setQueue (nice catch). Added checks in test case. Also updated AppSchedulingInfo as it also maintains the queue name.
